attributes.description: Files >= 100 MB sitting in user-writable / non-system locations (Users, Temp, ProgramData, root of secondary drives). At this size threshold the noise drops off sharply — what remains is the shortlist of files genuinely worth stealing: full database exports, VM disk images, big archives of source code or PII, video captures, etc. Cross-reference with the Browser dashboard's downloads, the LNK dashboard's opened-files panel, and the Shellbags removable-media panel to figure out where each one came from and where it went.

kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON: {"filter":[],"highlightAll":true,"indexRefName":"kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON.index","query":{"language":"kuery","query":"file.size >= 104857600 and file.path : (*\\\\Users\\\\* or *\\\\Temp\\\\* or *\\\\ProgramData\\\\* or *\\\\Intel\\\\* or *\\\\PerfLogs\\\\*) and not file.path : (*\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\Microsoft\\\\* or *\\\\Microsoft\\\\Windows\\\\* or *\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\Packages\\\\* or *\\\\WindowsApps\\\\*)"},"version":true}

attributes.title: Filesystem: Large Files (>= 100 MB) in User-Writable Locations
